summary: FRONTEND WON'T COMPILE. LpMaterialListPanel.jsx has a syntax error at line 488 ('king-wider text-red-700"') — E1 shipped a broken edit: the new three-verb amber-item JSX (Verify / Wrong wall / Not present) was appended AFTER the component's closing `}` on line 487, leaving a duplicate old amber-block (lines 435-474 with only the single Verify button) and a truncated/orphaned tail (lines 488-553). Webpack shows 'SyntaxError: Missing semicolon (488:10)' and the app renders only the red 'Compiled with problems' overlay. Zero user-flow testing was possible — could not verify any of the 6 requested steps (three-verb buttons, wall picker, relocation refetch/3D chase-box move, revert, Not present, audit timeline).

ui_bugs.issue: File is structurally broken. The component function closes at line 487 (`}`), then lines 488-553 contain an orphaned duplicate of the amber field-verify + 3D blocks whose first line is truncated to `king-wider text-red-700"` (missing everything before `tra`). Result: React compile fails ('SyntaxError: Missing semicolon (488:10)') and the ONLY amber-item UI actually inside the component (lines 435-474) is the old single-Verify-button version — the new Verify/Wrong wall/Not present three-verb buttons live in dead code after the closing brace.

action_items: Fix /app/frontend/src/components/estimate/LpMaterialListPanel.jsx: delete the old amber-field-verify JSX block (lines ~435-474 — the single-button version) and splice the new three-verb block (currently orphaned at lines 488-540) INTO the component before the ` ); }` on line 485-487. Restore the truncated line 488 (should begin with something like `className="... bg-red-700 text-white ..."` on the removed button).

After compile is restored, re-run this playbook: three verbs visible; Wrong wall → select excludes 'right' → pick back → Relocated badge + back-wall badge; 3D chimney chase renders on back wall (label 'relocated (was right)'); revert; Not present on OSC row → ✕ Removed + '1 unconfirmed' note; revert. Audit Timeline in BrandingAdmin (Events button, expand/collapse, corner.relocated/reset entries).
critical_code_review_comments: LpMaterialListPanel.jsx has DUPLICATED amber-field-verify + 3D-preview JSX (lines 435-487 vs 488-553). Even if the syntax error is patched, the duplication itself will render the amber card twice and re-mount HouseModel3D twice, doubling GL contexts and API refetches.

The truncated line 488 suggests E1's edit tool overwrote the first ~40 characters of a className line — worth checking whether an editor 'insert-at-cursor' was applied without deleting the prior region.
No compile-check gate: E1 shipped without running `yarn build` or even a basic page load — every subsequent test iteration would be blocked. Add a pre-finish lint/build step.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Estimate 8f95c9c2-add9-416a-92f3-786a4ea2ce83 was NOT modified by this run (no verbs applied — page never rendered past the red compile overlay), so no revert cleanup is needed. Once the JSX is fixed, follow the 6-step playbook exactly. IMPORTANT: resume-only in AI Measure modal; do NOT start new AI runs. Revert every verb applied before finishing.
rca of the issue: Root cause: LpMaterialListPanel.jsx line 488 begins mid-string with `king-wider text-red-700"` — Babel parser hits an identifier where it expects a statement separator, fails with 'Missing semicolon (488:10)'. The functional root cause is that E1 appended the new three-verb JSX (intended to replace the old single-Verify block in the amber-items .map) AFTER the component's closing `}` on line 487 rather than substituting it in place, and additionally the very first line of the appended block was truncated (probably by a bad search_replace where old_str partially overlapped a className token). Reproduction: `curl` the frontend / open any route → red 'Compiled with problems' overlay with the exact error. Mitigation: (a) delete lines 435-487's inner amber-map body and replace with the well-formed three-verb version from lines 488-540; (b) drop the orphaned second copy entirely so the file ends at the single component `}`; (c) yarn lint + reload to confirm no other duplication remains.
